intro_text: Hi there! I'm Rishika, and today we're going to master fractions together! 🍕 From pizza slices to recipe measurements, fractions are everywhere — let's unlock their secrets!
complete_message: Fantastic work! You've mastered Working with Fractions! 🎉

explain_questions.question: What is a proper fraction? Give an example.
explain_questions.question_spoken: What is a proper fraction? Give an example.

steps.t: A fraction has two parts: numerator (top) and denominator (bottom).
steps.s: A fraction has two parts: the numerator on top and the denominator on the bottom.

steps.t: A PROPER fraction: numerator is LESS than denominator.
steps.s: A proper fraction is one where the numerator is less than the denominator.
For example, 3 over 5. Since 3 is less than 5, it is a proper fraction. Its value is always less than 1.

nudges: Compare the top number with the bottom number.
7 is the numerator and 9 is the denominator. Which is bigger?
7 is less than 9, so yes, 7/9 is a proper fraction!
explain_questions.anim_svg: <g id="q1s0" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="28" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">Fraction = numerator (top) / denominator (bottom)</text></g><g id="q1s1" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="68" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">Proper fraction: numerator < denominator</text></g><g id="q1s2" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="108" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">Example: 3/5 (3 < 5) → proper fraction</text></g><g id="q1ans" opacity="0"><rect x="40" y="148" width="320" height="24" rx="7" fill="#eef2eb" stroke="#6b4c2a" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="210" y="163" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Share Tech Mono" font-size="12" font-weight="bold" fill="#7a8c6e">Yes, 7/9 is a proper fraction</text></g>

explain_questions.question: Convert the mixed number 2 3/4 into an improper fraction.
explain_questions.question_spoken: Convert the mixed number 2 and 3 over 4 into an improper fraction.

steps.t: Mixed number = whole part + fraction part. Here: 2 and 3/4.
steps.s: A mixed number has a whole part and a fraction part. Here we have 2 and 3 over 4.

steps.t: Multiply whole number by denominator: 2 x 4 = 8.
steps.s: Multiply the whole number 2 by the denominator 4 to get 8.
Now add the numerator 3 to get 11. So 2 and 3 over 4 equals 11 over 4.

explain_questions.confirm_question_spoken: Convert 3 and 2 over 5 to an improper fraction.
nudges: Multiply the whole number by the denominator first.
3 times 5 equals 15. Now add the numerator.
15 plus 2 equals 17. So the answer is 17/5!
explain_questions.anim_svg: <g id="q2s0" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="28" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">Mixed number 2 3/4 = whole + fraction</text></g><g id="q2s1" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="68" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">Step 1: 2 x 4 = 8</text></g><g id="q2s2" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="108" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">Step 2: 8 + 3 = 11 → Answer: 11/4</text></g><g id="q2ans" opacity="0"><rect x="40" y="148" width="320" height="24" rx="7" fill="#eef2eb" stroke="#6b4c2a" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="210" y="163" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Share Tech Mono" font-size="12" font-weight="bold" fill="#7a8c6e">17/5</text></g>

explain_questions.question: Find equivalent fractions of 2/3 by multiplying numerator and denominator by 2 and 3.
explain_questions.question_spoken: Find equivalent fractions of 2 over 3 by multiplying numerator and denominator by 2 and then by 3.

steps.t: Equivalent fractions: same value, different numerator/denominator.
steps.s: Equivalent fractions have the same value but different numerators and denominators.
Multiply both numerator and denominator by 2 to get 4 over 6.
Multiply both by 3 to get 6 over 9. So 2/3, 4/6 and 6/9 are all equivalent.

explain_questions.confirm_question_spoken: Write one equivalent fraction of 3 over 5.
nudges: Multiply both top and bottom by the same number.
Try multiplying by 2: what do you get?
3x2=6 and 5x2=10, so 6/10 is an equivalent fraction of 3/5!
explain_questions.anim_svg: <g id="q3s0" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="28" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">Equivalent fractions: same value, different form</text></g><g id="q3s1" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="68" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">2/3 x (2/2) = 4/6</text></g><g id="q3s2" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="108" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">2/3 x (3/3) = 6/9</text></g><g id="q3ans" opacity="0"><rect x="40" y="148" width="320" height="24" rx="7" fill="#eef2eb" stroke="#6b4c2a" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="210" y="163" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Share Tech Mono" font-size="12" font-weight="bold" fill="#7a8c6e">6/10 (or 9/15, 12/20...)</text></g>

explain_questions.question: Compare 3/4 and 5/6. Which is greater?
explain_questions.question_spoken: Compare 3 over 4 and 5 over 6. Which is greater?

steps.t: To compare unlike fractions, find the LCM of denominators.
steps.s: To compare fractions with different denominators, find the LCM of 4 and 6.
The LCM of 4 and 6 is 12. So 3 over 4 becomes 9 over 12, and 5 over 6 becomes 10 over 12.
Comparing 9 over 12 and 10 over 12, we see 10 is greater. So 5 over 6 is greater than 3 over 4.

explain_questions.confirm_question_spoken: Which is greater: 2 over 3 or 3 over 5?
nudges: Find the LCM of 3 and 5 first.
LCM of 3 and 5 is 15. Convert both fractions to have denominator 15.
2/3 = 10/15 and 3/5 = 9/15. Since 10 > 9, the answer is 2/3!
explain_questions.anim_svg: <g id="q4s0" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="28" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">Compare unlike fractions using LCM</text></g><g id="q4s1" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="68" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">LCM(4,6)=12 → 3/4=9/12, 5/6=10/12</text></g><g id="q4s2" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="108" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">9/12 < 10/12 → 5/6 > 3/4</text></g><g id="q4ans" opacity="0"><rect x="40" y="148" width="320" height="24" rx="7" fill="#eef2eb" stroke="#6b4c2a" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="210" y="163" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Share Tech Mono" font-size="12" font-weight="bold" fill="#7a8c6e">5/6 is greater</text></g>

explain_questions.question: Add 1/4 + 2/3.

steps.t: Unlike fractions: find LCM of denominators. LCM(4,3) = 12.
steps.s: These are unlike fractions. Find the LCM of 4 and 3, which is 12.
Convert 1 over 4 to 3 over 12, and 2 over 3 to 8 over 12.
Now add the numerators: 3 plus 8 equals 11. The answer is 11 over 12.

nudges: Find the LCM of 3 and 4.
LCM of 3 and 4 is 12. Convert both fractions.
explain_questions.anim_svg: <g id="q5s0" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="28" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">LCM(4, 3) = 12</text></g><g id="q5s1" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="68" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">1/4 = 3/12 and 2/3 = 8/12</text></g><g id="q5s2" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="108" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">3/12 + 8/12 = 11/12</text></g><g id="q5ans" opacity="0"><rect x="40" y="148" width="320" height="24" rx="7" fill="#eef2eb" stroke="#6b4c2a" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="210" y="163" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Share Tech Mono" font-size="12" font-weight="bold" fill="#7a8c6e">11/12</text></g>

explain_questions.question: Subtract 3/8 from 5/6.

steps.s: Find the LCM of 6 and 8. The LCM is 24.
Convert 5 over 6 to 20 over 24, and 3 over 8 to 9 over 24.
Subtract the numerators: 20 minus 9 equals 11. The answer is 11 over 24.

nudges: Find LCM of 4 and 8.
LCM of 4 and 8 is 8. Convert 1/4 to have denominator 8.
explain_questions.anim_svg: <g id="q6s0" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="28" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">LCM(6, 8) = 24</text></g><g id="q6s1" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="68" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">5/6 = 20/24 and 3/8 = 9/24</text></g><g id="q6s2" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="108" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">20/24 - 9/24 = 11/24</text></g><g id="q6ans" opacity="0"><rect x="40" y="148" width="320" height="24" rx="7" fill="#eef2eb" stroke="#6b4c2a" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="210" y="163" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Share Tech Mono" font-size="12" font-weight="bold" fill="#7a8c6e">11/24</text></g>

explain_questions.question: Multiply 2/3 x 3/5.

steps.t: To multiply fractions: multiply numerators, multiply denominators.
steps.s: To multiply fractions, multiply the numerators together and the denominators together.
Numerators: 2 times 3 equals 6. Denominators: 3 times 5 equals 15.
The result is 6 over 15. Simplify by dividing both by 3 to get 2 over 5.

nudges: Multiply numerators together and denominators together.
3 x 2 = 6 and 4 x 9 = 36. So you get 6/36.
Simplify 6/36 by dividing both by 6. Answer is 1/6!
explain_questions.anim_svg: <g id="q7s0" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="28" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">Multiply: (num x num) / (den x den)</text></g><g id="q7s1" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="68" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">2x3=6, 3x5=15 → 6/15</text></g><g id="q7s2" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="108" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">Simplify 6/15 ÷ 3 = 2/5</text></g><g id="q7ans" opacity="0"><rect x="40" y="148" width="320" height="24" rx="7" fill="#eef2eb" stroke="#6b4c2a" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="210" y="163" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Share Tech Mono" font-size="12" font-weight="bold" fill="#7a8c6e">2/5</text></g>

explain_questions.question: Divide 3/4 by 2/5.

steps.t: To divide fractions: multiply by the reciprocal of the divisor.
steps.s: To divide a fraction, multiply it by the reciprocal of the divisor.
The reciprocal of 2 over 5 is 5 over 2. Now multiply 3 over 4 by 5 over 2.
3 times 5 is 15, and 4 times 2 is 8. So the answer is 15 over 8, which equals 1 and 7 over 8.

nudges: Flip the second fraction and multiply.
Reciprocal of 4/5 is 5/4. So now multiply 2/3 by 5/4.
explain_questions.anim_svg: <g id="q8s0" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="28" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">Division = multiply by reciprocal</text></g><g id="q8s1" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="68" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">3/4 ÷ 2/5 = 3/4 x 5/2</text></g><g id="q8s2" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="108" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">15/8 = 1 and 7/8</text></g><g id="q8ans" opacity="0"><rect x="40" y="148" width="320" height="24" rx="7" fill="#eef2eb" stroke="#6b4c2a" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="210" y="163" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Share Tech Mono" font-size="12" font-weight="bold" fill="#7a8c6e">15/8</text></g>

explain_questions.question: Riya ate 2/5 of a pizza. What fraction is left?
explain_questions.question_spoken: Riya ate 2 over 5 of a pizza. What fraction is left?

steps.s: The total pizza is 1 whole. Riya ate 2 over 5 of it.
To find what is left, subtract 2 over 5 from 1. Write 1 as 5 over 5.
5 over 5 minus 2 over 5 equals 3 over 5. So 3 over 5 of the pizza is left.

explain_questions.confirm_question: Arjun finished 3/7 of his homework. What fraction is remaining?
explain_questions.confirm_question_spoken: Arjun finished 3 over 7 of his homework. What fraction is remaining?
nudges: Total work is 1 whole. Subtract the completed fraction.
Write 1 as 7/7.
7/7 - 3/7 = 4/7 remaining!
explain_questions.anim_svg: <g id="q9s0" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="28" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">Total = 1, Eaten = 2/5</text></g><g id="q9s1" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="68" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">Remaining = 1 - 2/5 = 5/5 - 2/5</text></g><g id="q9s2" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="108" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">5/5 - 2/5 = 3/5</text></g><g id="q9ans" opacity="0"><rect x="40" y="148" width="320" height="24" rx="7" fill="#eef2eb" stroke="#6b4c2a" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="210" y="163" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Share Tech Mono" font-size="12" font-weight="bold" fill="#7a8c6e">3/5</text></g>

explain_questions.question: A rope is 7 1/2 m long. It is cut into pieces of 3/4 m each. How many pieces?
explain_questions.question_spoken: A rope is 7 and a half metres long. It is cut into pieces of 3 over 4 metres each. How many pieces are formed?

steps.s: First convert 7 and a half to an improper fraction. 7 times 2 plus 1 equals 15. So 7 and a half equals 15 over 2.

steps.t: Number of pieces = total length / piece length = (15/2) / (3/4).
steps.s: Divide the total length by the piece length. That is 15 over 2 divided by 3 over 4.
Flip 3 over 4 to get 4 over 3. Multiply: 15 times 4 is 60, 2 times 3 is 6. So 60 over 6 equals 10 pieces.

explain_questions.confirm_question: How many pieces of 1/3 m can be cut from a rope of 4 m?
explain_questions.confirm_question_spoken: How many pieces of 1 over 3 metres can be cut from a rope of 4 metres?
nudges: Divide total length by piece length: 4 divided by 1/3.
Dividing by 1/3 is same as multiplying by 3.
4 x 3 = 12 pieces!
explain_questions.anim_svg: <g id="q10s0" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="28" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">7 1/2 = 15/2 m</text></g><g id="q10s1" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="68" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">Pieces = (15/2) ÷ (3/4)</text></g><g id="q10s2" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="108" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">= 15/2 x 4/3 = 60/6 = 10</text></g><g id="q10ans" opacity="0"><rect x="40" y="148" width="320" height="24" rx="7" fill="#eef2eb" stroke="#6b4c2a" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="210" y="163" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Share Tech Mono" font-size="12" font-weight="bold" fill="#7a8c6e">10 pieces</text></g>

practice_questions.question: Convert 5 2/3 to an improper fraction.
practice_questions.question_spoken: Convert 5 and 2 over 3 to an improper fraction.
steps: Multiply whole number by denominator: 5 x 3 = 15.
practice_questions.trick: Rishika Trick: (whole x denominator + numerator) / denominator. Always works in one step!
practice_questions.alt: Think of it as 5 full groups of 3 thirds (=15 thirds) plus 2 more thirds = 17 thirds.

practice_questions.question: Convert 11/4 to a mixed number.
practice_questions.question_spoken: Convert 11 over 4 to a mixed number.
practice_questions.trick: Rishika Trick: Divide numerator by denominator. Quotient is whole part, remainder is new numerator!

practice_questions.question: Are 2/3 and 8/12 equivalent fractions?
practice_questions.question_spoken: Are 2 over 3 and 8 over 12 equivalent fractions?
steps: Both equal 24, so yes they are equivalent.
practice_questions.trick: Rishika Trick: Cross multiply. If products are equal, fractions are equivalent!
practice_questions.alt: Simplify 8/12 by dividing both by 4: 8/12 = 2/3. Same as original, so equivalent.

practice_questions.question: Which is greater: 4/7 or 5/9?
practice_questions.question_spoken: Which is greater: 4 over 7 or 5 over 9?
practice_questions.trick: Rishika Trick: Cross multiply! 4x9=36 and 5x7=35. Since 36>35, the fraction on the left (4/7) is greater!
practice_questions.alt: Convert to decimals: 4/7 ≈ 0.571 and 5/9 ≈ 0.556. Since 0.571 > 0.556, 4/7 is greater.

practice_questions.question: Add 2/5 + 3/10.
practice_questions.trick: Rishika Trick: When one denominator is a multiple of the other, just convert the smaller one!

practice_questions.question: Subtract 1/6 from 1/2.
practice_questions.trick: Rishika Trick: LCM of 2 and 6 is 6. Convert 1/2 to 3/6. Then subtract easily!

practice_questions.question: Multiply 4/5 x 15/16.
practice_questions.trick: Rishika Trick: Cancel before multiplying! 4 and 16 share factor 4 (giving 1 and 4). 5 and 15 share factor 5 (giving 1 and 3). So 1x3 / 1x4 = 3/4.
practice_questions.alt: 4/5 x 15/16 = 60/80. Divide both by HCF(60,80)=20 to get 3/4.

practice_questions.question: Divide 5/6 by 10/3.
practice_questions.trick: Rishika Trick: Cancel before multiplying! 5 and 10 share factor 5. 3 and 6 share factor 3. That gives 1/2 x 1/2 = 1/4.

practice_questions.question: Neha had 3 1/4 litres of juice. She drank 1 2/3 litres. How much is left?
practice_questions.question_spoken: Neha had 3 and a quarter litres of juice. She drank 1 and 2 over 3 litres. How much is left?
practice_questions.trick: Rishika Trick: Convert mixed numbers to improper fractions first, then subtract. Much easier!

practice_questions.question: What is 3/7 of 49?
practice_questions.trick: Rishika Trick: Divide first then multiply! 49 ÷ 7 = 7, then 7 x 3 = 21. Much simpler!

practice_questions.question: Arrange in ascending order: 1/2, 1/3, 1/4, 1/6.
practice_questions.question_spoken: Arrange in ascending order: 1 over 2, 1 over 3, 1 over 4, 1 over 6.
steps: All have numerator 1. For unit fractions, larger denominator = smaller value.
practice_questions.trick: Rishika Trick: For unit fractions (1/n), the fraction with the BIGGEST denominator is SMALLEST!

practice_questions.question: A tank is 5/8 full. After adding water it becomes 7/8 full. What fraction was added?
practice_questions.question_spoken: A tank is 5 over 8 full. After adding water it becomes 7 over 8 full. What fraction of the tank was added?
practice_questions.trick: Rishika Trick: Same denominators means just subtract numerators. 7-5=2, so 2/8 = 1/4!
practice_questions.alt: 7/8 - 5/8 = 2/8. Divide numerator and denominator by 2 to simplify: 1/4.

practice_questions.question: Find the product: 2 1/3 x 1 1/2.
practice_questions.question_spoken: Find the product of 2 and 1 over 3 multiplied by 1 and 1 over 2.
practice_questions.trick: Rishika Trick: Convert to improper fractions first, then multiply. Cancel 3 in numerator and denominator: 7/3 x 3/2 = 7/2.

practice_questions.question: A recipe needs 2/3 cup of sugar for 1 cake. How much sugar is needed for 6 cakes?
practice_questions.question_spoken: A recipe needs 2 over 3 cup of sugar for 1 cake. How much sugar is needed for 6 cakes?
practice_questions.trick: Rishika Trick: 6 x 2/3 = (6/3) x 2 = 2 x 2 = 4. Simplify first then multiply!

practice_questions.question: Divide 4 1/2 by 1 1/2.
practice_questions.question_spoken: Divide 4 and a half by 1 and a half.
practice_questions.trick: Rishika Trick: When dividing mixed numbers, convert to improper fractions first. Here 9/2 ÷ 3/2 = 9/3 = 3 (the 2s cancel)!
practice_questions.alt: 4.5 ÷ 1.5 = 3. You can also think: how many 1.5s fit in 4.5? Answer: 3.
